name: GAX infrastructure evolution
current_usage_summary: Prototype establishes pattern: gax CLI (invocation) → gaxd HTTP sidecar (localhost:9477) → policy/caps/audit → adapters. Roadmap phases: harden auth (OAuth device, keychain, macaroons), OPA, OTel, plan DAG; ecosystem (OpenAPI manifests, MCP bridge); enterprise (multi-tenant vault, SPIFFE, hosted gaxd, compliance exports). Infrastructure evolves from 'developer daemon' to 'organizational agent control plane' analogous to API gateway + secrets manager + policy engine.
typical_stack: Phase 0 (now): Python gax/gaxd, JWT caps (dev secret), JSONL audit in ~/.gax/, YAML manifests. Phase 1: OAuth, keychain, OPA bundles, OTel. Phase 2: MCP bridge, kubectl/aws manifests. Phase 3: Vault integration, hosted gaxd SaaS, SPIFFE mTLS between agent host and gaxd.
auth_and_identity_model: Evolution: dev tenant in config.json → per-user OAuth refresh in vault → cap mint with attenuation (command, scope, TTL, budget) → macaroons for delegation → workload identity for gaxd itself.
context_and_token_behavior: Projection service in gaxd enforces surface=model|human|full. Registry search index for lazy discovery. plan run batches steps server-side.
operational_pain_points: gaxd SPOF unless HA. Cap revocation lag if only JWT TTL. Manifest signing/trust chain needed for third-party commands.
developer_experience_friction: Operating gaxd clusters new skill for platform teams. Manifest versioning semver per command.
gax_mapping: N/A—this item describes GAX infra itself.
migration_phases: Org installs hosted gaxd → connects IdP → publishes internal manifest bundle → agents receive caps via SSO portal plugin → SIEM ingests audit stream → gradually disconnect direct SaaS from laptops.
adapter_or_bridge_strategy: Adapter types: exec, http, mock, mcp (roadmap). Manifest generator from OpenAPI reduces ops toil.
breaking_changes_and_mitigations: JWT dev secret → production keys: rotation playbook. Envelope v1 → v2: version field already in schema.
infrastructure_changes: Components: gaxd replicas, Redis for revocation, vault, OPA bundle CDN, manifest git repo, OTel collector, optional WASM sandbox for jq/plan. Network policies: agents → gaxd only; gaxd → SaaS APIs. Cost: small always-on service vs per-MCP-server process proliferation.
deployment_topology: Tier 1 solo: single local gaxd. Tier 2 team: gaxd in shared dev cluster. Tier 3 enterprise: multi-region gaxd, private link to vault, per-BU tenants. Tier 4 vendor: ISV hosts gaxd multi-tenant for customers (SaaS control plane).
adoption_drivers: Platform teams want one knob for agent permissions across Claude/Cursor/custom bots. FinOps ties caps to API quota budgets.
adoption_blockers: Another infrastructure product to operate. Overlap with API gateway and PAM tools—requires clear positioning.
adoption_timeline_estimate: Prototype ops now; production-grade hosted gaxd 24–36 months without major vendor partnership.
historical_parallel: HashiCorp Vault + Consul + Envoy mesh combined for human ops; GAX consolidates for agent ops.
